title: Disability Rights Groups - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Disability Rights Groups 20 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Blindness ARC OF BENTON COUNTY ADVOCACY AND DAY SUPPORTS ACTIVITIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides advocacy services, an employment training program, and supported recreational activities such as art instruction, poetry classes, bowling, game nights, movies, and others. A community calendar with listings of activities can be found on the website. If one-on-one assistance is required for recreational activities, individuals must provide their own staff. 928 NW Beca Avenue Corvallis, OR 97330 Eligibility: Recreational activites: Ages 18 and older with intellectual or developmental disabilites and their families All other services: Individuals with intellectual or developmental disabilities and their families. Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 602-9225 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK BEND OFFICE COMMUNITY EDUCATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Educates members of the medical community, professionals, self-advocates, caregivers, and anyone in the greater community who is interested in making their home or place of business more welcoming for people living with disabilities. The program can be tailored to agency or group needs. Classes combine the real life expertise of families with accurate information on disability. The length of training can vary based on need. Full day trainings are also available. Topics that have been covered include, Creating a Welcoming Environment, Individual Education Plans (IEP), Fine Motor, Speech and Language, Financial Planning for People with Disabilities, Person Centered Planning, and Healthy Eating Habits and Special Diets. All classes provide families with free on-site child care. 2525 NE Twin Knolls Drive Suite 7 Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm. Hours vary for classes. Email (541) 548-8559 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK KLAMATH FALLS OFFICE COMMUNITY EDUCATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Educates members of the medical community, professionals, self-advocates, caregivers, and anyone in the greater community who is interested in making their home or place of business more welcoming for people living with disabilities. Topics that have been covered include, Creating a Welcoming Environment, Individual Education Plans (IEP), Fine Motor, Speech and Language, Financial Planning for People with Disabilities, Person Centered Planning, and Healthy Eating Habits and Special Diets. All classes provide families with free on-site child care. 2701 Foothills Boulevard Klamath Falls, OR 97601 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: By appointment only Email (541) 548-8559 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK WARM SPRINGS OFFICE COMMUNITY EDUCATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Educates members of the medical community, professionals, self-advocates, caregivers, and anyone in the greater community who is interested in making their home or place of business more welcoming for people living with disabilities. The program can be tailored to agency or group needs. Classes combine the real life expertise of families with accurate information on disability. The length of training can vary based on need. Email (541) 548-8559 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CITY OF PORTLAND 311 CITY AND COUNTY INFORMATION AND REFERRAL Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Information and referral services to all City of Portland Bureaus, Multnomah County Departments, and other local government services. Also provides information about disability rights and resolves issues about ADA accessibility for the City of Portland. 1120 SW 5th Avenue Suite 114 Portland, OR 97204 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Phone or email: 7 days per week 7am-8pm (closed for federal holidays) Email (503) 823-4000 311 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DISABILITY RIGHTS OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides advocacy and limited legal services to people with disabilities. DRO's individual advocacy focuses on providing callers with information and materials about their rights to help them advocate for themselves. Occasionally, Disability Rights Oregon will engage in litigation to protect the rights of Oregonians under applicable federal and state laws. DRO does not provide the following services: -Representation in criminal or family law matters -Help finding employment, housing, or financial assistance -Case management -Assistance for short term disability issues -Transportation to doctor's office -Applying for or appealing denial of Social Security disability benefits -Estates and trusts -Becoming the guardian of a person with a disability -Out-of-state issues -Workers' compensation -Medical malpractice or personal injury -Consumer or bankruptcy issues -Anything that is not the wish of the person with the disability 900 SW 5th Avenue Suite 1800 Portland, OR 97204 Eligibility: Individuals with disabilities who have legal issues related to their disabilities Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-11am and 1pm-4pm Email (503) 243-2081 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DISABLED AMERICAN VETERANS VA REGIONAL OFFICE OREGON DISABLED AMERICAN VETERANS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps individuals access benefits and services earned through military service and provided by the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) and other government agencies such as healthcare, disability compensation, education, employment, and financial assistance. Provides additional support services like job finding assistance, Medicare information and counseling, transportation to medical appointments, emergency financial grants, support groups, volunteer opportunities, advocacy, and more. 100 SW Main Street Room 212a Portland, OR 97204 Eligibility: Veterans or dependents of veterans, with a focus on those with disabilities Hours: -National Call Center: 6am-1pm -Local Office Phone: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 8am-3:30pm, Wednesday 8am-noon -Local Office Walk in: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 8am-2:30pm, Wednesday 8am-noon Email (503) 412-4750 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING BURNS INFORMATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides technical assistance, Website More Details NATIONAL FEDERATION OF THE BLIND Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps individuals achieve independence and equality by providing information and referral, employment information, literature, scholarship programs, and a blindness-related adaptive equipment store. The Free White Cane Program provides rigid fiberglass canes. 200 E Wells Street Baltimore, MD 21230 Eligibility: -Primarily blind and visually impaired individuals, but sighted persons may also join -Free White Cane Program: Must be blind or visually impaired, must be requesting the cane for personal use, and it must have been more than 6 months since a previous white cane request Hours: Varies by chapter National office: Monday-Friday 5am-2pm Email (541) 653-9153 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON COMMISSION FOR THE BLIND EUGENE FIELD OFFICE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides vocational rehabilitation, independent living skills training, career exploration and general counseling for legally blind adults. Also provides independent living services for visually impaired individuals. Includes orientation and mobility training, techniques of daily living, Braille, counseling, support groups, vocational training, job development, and transition services for blind high school students. Also offers a selection of adaptive items for people with visual impairments. 541 Willamette Avenue #408 Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Varies by service; adults certified legally blind or diagnosed with condition leading to blindness, or age 55 years and above and unable to drive or read newspaper with glasses. Age 55 and older for independent living services. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 686-7990 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON COMMISSION FOR THE BLIND MEDFORD FIELD OFFICE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides vocational rehabilitation, independent living skills training, career exploration and general counseling for legally blind adults. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 378-8478 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES SENIORS AND PEOPLE WITH DISABILITIES OREGON DISABILITIES COMMISSION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Identifies and hears the concerns of individuals with disabilities and uses the information to prioritize public policy issues which should be addressed, educates and advises the Oregon Department of Human Services (ODHS), the Governor, the Legislative Assembly and appropriate state agency administrators on how public policy can be improved to meet the needs of individuals with disabilities. 500 Summer Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (800) 282-8096 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES VOCATIONAL REHABILITATION ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICE OREGON STATE REHABILITATION COUNCIL Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The purpose of the Oregon State Rehabilitation Council is to provide the Vocational Rehabilitation (VR) with an external, consumer-oriented perspective. The Council advises VR on the needs of Oregonians with disabilities concerning programs, policy, services and other issues. 500 Summer Street NE Suite E87 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: No standard hours (503) 945-6256 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ARC OF BENTON COUNTY ADVOCACY AND DAY SUPPORTS ACTIVITIES CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK COMMUNITY EDUCATION PROGRAM CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK COMMUNITY EDUCATION PROGRAM CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK COMMUNITY EDUCATION PROGRAM CITY OF PORTLAND 311 CITY AND COUNTY INFORMATION AND REFERRAL DISABILITY RIGHTS OREGON DISABILITY RIGHTS OREGON DISABLED AMERICAN VETERANS VA REGIONAL OFFICE OREGON DISABLED AMERICAN VETERANS EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING INFORMATION SERVICES EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING INFORMATION SERVICES EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING INFORMATION SERVICES EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING INFORMATION SERVICES EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING INFORMATION SERVICES OREGON COMMISSION FOR THE BLIND EUGENE FIELD OFFICE OREGON COMMISSION FOR THE BLIND MEDFORD FIELD OFFICE OREGON COMMISSION FOR THE BLIND OREGON COMMISSION FOR THE BLIND OREGON COMMISSION FOR THE BLIND REDMOND FIELD OFFICE OREGON COMMISSION FOR THE BLIND SALEM FIELD OFFICE OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES OREGON DISABILITIES COMMISSION OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES OREGON STATE REHABILITATION COUNCIL
